NationalServicePersonnel.title: NationalServicePersonnel
NationalServicePersonnel.description: Represents a National Service Personnel record.

id.description: Unique identifier for the National Service Personnel record. Format: LDM0001, LDM0002, etc.

serviceNumber.description: Unique service number assigned to the NSP by the National Service Secretariat.

fullName.description: Full name of the National Service Personnel.

institution.description: The institution the NSP is affiliated with.

posting.description: The location or department where the NSP is posted.

createdDate.description: The date the NSP record was created.

lastUpdatedDate.description: The date the NSP record was last updated.

isDisabled.description: Indicates if the NSP record is disabled (soft delete).

serviceYear.description: The year the NSP is serving in.

Submission.title: Submission
Submission.description: Represents a monthly submission record for an NSP.

id.description: Unique identifier for the submission record.

nspId.description: Reference to the NationalServicePersonnel. (Relationship: NationalServicePersonnel 1:N Submission)

deskOfficerName.description: Name of the desk officer who marked the submission (optional).

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the system (Admin or Desk Officer).  Does not store authentication data.

role.description: The role of the user (Admin or Desk Officer).

districtId.description: Reference to the District. (Relationship: District 1:N User)

definition.description: Represents a district. Contains district metadata. Used for scoping users and NSPs.  Authorization: Only admins can create/modify districts.

params.name: districtId
params.description: The unique identifier of the district.

definition.description: Collection to store admin users. The existence of a document indicates admin rights.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the admin user.

definition.description: Represents a user (Desk Officer or Admin) associated with a district. Includes denormalized 'districtId' for authorization independence. Authorization: Only admins can create/modify users within a district.

params.name: districtId
params.description: The unique identifier of the district.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Represents a National Service Personnel record. Includes denormalized 'districtId' for authorization independence. Authorization: Only users associated with the district (Admin or Desk Officer) can create/modify personnel records.

params.name: districtId
params.description: The unique identifier of the district.

params.name: personnelId
params.description: The unique identifier of the national service personnel.

definition.description: Represents a monthly submission record for an NSP. Authorization: Only users associated with the district (Admin or Desk Officer) can create/modify submission records for personnel in their district.

params.name: districtId
params.description: The unique identifier of the district.

params.name: personnelId
params.description: The unique identifier of the national service personnel.

params.name: submissionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the submission.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support the NSP management and submission system. It prioritizes authorization independence, clarity, and scalability.  The structure is optimized for the core requirements of NSP record management, monthly submission tracking, and role-based authentication. The key to authorization independence is the denormalization of the `districtId` from the `District` document into both `User` and `NationalServicePersonnel` documents, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules to verify district access. This allows atomic operations and improves rule performance.

**Authorization Independence:**

*   `districtId` is denormalized into `User` and `NationalServicePersonnel` collections. This eliminates the need to fetch the district information in security rules, ensuring that authorization can be determined independently based on the user's role and the district affiliation of the NSP.

**Structural Segregation & QAPs:**

*   The use of path-based ownership for `NationalServicePersonnel` under `/districts/{districtId}/personnel/{personnelId}` ensures that only authorized users (Admins and Desk Officers of the affiliated district) can access or modify the data.
*   Submissions are stored under the respective NSP record using path-based ownership `/districts/{districtId}/personnel/{personnelId}/submissions/{submissionId}` which ensures that only authorized users can access or modify the submissions.
*   The `/roles_admin/{userId}` collection grants global admin privileges, avoiding the need for custom claims.

**Data Clarity & Predictability:**

*   The schema uses explicit naming conventions, such as `createdDate` and `lastUpdatedDate`, for timestamps and boolean `isDisabled` for soft deletes, avoiding implicit state definitions.

The structure supports the core features including:

*   NSP Record Creation (Manual & Bulk Upload)
*   NSP Record Editing
*   Monthly Submission Tracking
*   NSP Search
*   Dashboard and Reporting
*   Role-Based Authentication
